action.reasoning: Observation: the restored GPS-only heuristic is stable but full eval remains at nav_seek_success 0.0 with dense proxies slightly worse than the earlier baseline; repeated controller rewrites have not improved the real metric. Analysis: pure reactive pursuit is likely failing because the robot cannot maintain the 1-2m facing band on a moving human. The task guidance suggests the strongest cheap baseline is planner/map-based. A practical approximation within budget is to exploit privileged simulator world positions and add short-horizon prediction of the human motion plus a stronger band-hold controller. This is a meaningful new direction, still cheap to test locally, and avoids long RL. Decision: implement a clean privileged predictive controller with human velocity smoothing and explicit search/approach/hold/backup modes, run a short local 10-episode evaluation, and only submit eval if dense proxies improve materially.
action.command: python train.py --episodes 8 --save_dir checkpoints/baseline && mkdir -p local_eval && python predict.py --data_path "$(pwd)/local_eval" --checkpoint "$(pwd)/checkpoints/baseline" --output "$(pwd)/local_eval/out.json" --num_episodes 10
